claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 16 years old and lives with me here in England. Can you help me work out if I'm eligible?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that context! I have information about you and Alex. Let me gather a bit more detail to assess eligibility properly.

Before I check the specific eligibility rules, I need to understand your circumstances better:

**Are you a British citizen, European Economic Area (EEA) citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm settled here. That's not an issue for me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Now let me read the eligibility criteria to ensure I'm applying the correct rules for your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming your settled status. Based on the eligibility information, I can see that for a 16-year-old child, there's an important condition I need to check with you.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** For example, is he in school, college, or an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! That's very helpful information. Let me check one more thing to be thorough.

**Does Alex live with you full-time, or does he live elsewhere (such as in student accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! I now have all the information I need to make a determination. Let me review the eligibility criteria against your circumstances.

Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed:

- You are settled in the UK ✓
- Alex is 16 years old and in approved education (A-levels at college) ✓
- Alex lives with you full-time ✓
- You are responsible for Alex's care and living costs ✓

All of these factors mean you meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 16 but still qualifies because he's in approved education (A-levels at college)
- The rules allow Child Benefit for children under 20 who remain in approved education or training
- Alex lives with you full-time, so you meet the responsibility requirements
- You have settled status in the UK, which meets the residency and immigration requirements

You should be able to proceed with your Child Benefit claim. If you have any other questions or circumstances change, feel free to contact the Child Benefit Office for additional support.

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and qualifies for Child Benefit because he is in approved education (A-levels at college). The eligibility rules state that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with you full-time and you are responsible for him, meeting the responsibility criteria. You have settled status in the UK, which satisfies the residency and immigration requirements.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. He meets all the necessary criteria: he is under 20, in approved education, living with you, and you have the appropriate immigration status to claim."]
